assumptions: The issue concerns hedge accounting under IFRS 9 at the consolidated financial statement level.
The identified foreign exchange exposure arises from an intragroup balance or transaction rather than from an external-party item.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposures to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk, including highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify net investment hedge accounting as a separate model for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation.
